name: Hot Orange Chutney
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Cups Firmly Packed Dark Brown Sugar
1/4 Cup Plus 1 Tablespoon Chopped Fresh Ginger
2 Red Bell Peppers, seeded and chopped
2 Green Bell Peppers, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the peel of oranges and lemon. Remove and discard all white pith.
2. Cut the fruit into quarters (reserve juices) and chop into cubes, discarding seeds.
3. Peel, core, and coarsely chop apples.
4. Transfer all fruit and juice to a heavy wide Dutch oven.
5. Add remaining ingredients.
6. Bring mixture to simmer over medium-high heat.
7. Reduce heat and continue simmering until the mixture is thick, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
8. Ladle hot chutney into clean, hot pint jars, leaving 1/2-inch from the top.
9. Run a plastic knife or spatula between the chutney and jar to release any air bubbles.
10. Clean the rim and threads of the jar with a damp cloth.
11. Seal with a new, scalded, very hot lid.
12. Repeat with remaining jars.
13. Transfer jars to gently simmering (180 degrees to 190 degrees) water bath and process for 10 minutes.
14. Let cool on a rack.
15. Test for seal.
16. Store in a cool dry place.
